Position Together for Progress

Project Details

Default fields only

Site Coordinates

WGS84 decimal degrees

Paste latitude and longitude as a single pair, for example 51.50806030158578, -0.12803349283074866. If access coordinates are left blank, the site marker becomes the routing origin.

Advanced Settings Collapsed by default
Walking colours
Cycling colours

Map Preview

Report-style draft output

Current mode Walking
Draft extent Local destinations focus
Legend items 0

Walking and cycling outputs are indicative active-travel network catchments generated for planning-stage accessibility review.

Amenities

Amenities & Connections

Connection Controls

Network edits

Select a drawing tool to add proposed routes, barriers or isochrone exclusion areas.

Drawing tips

Proposed walk, cycle, shared-use and bridge links are modelling assumptions for the local walking/cycling graph. Draw them so they cross or touch the road/path you want to connect to, then finish the drawing to trigger a refreshed walking or cycling catchment.

Barriers restrict crossed walking/cycling graph edges. Exclusion areas only mask the displayed isochrone fill and do not change the routed network.

Add Amenity Controls

Manual amenities
Amenities panel

Amenities

Rename, restyle, hide or remove plotted points.

Manual Map Editing

Manage proposed routes, barriers and exclusion areas.
